rationale.R1.1: The broad request to make the passage stronger centrally includes sharpening the contribution claim and resolving whether LIRA is framed as a router, advisor, or index-tuning tool.
rationale.R1.2: The artifact contains load-bearing internal tensions, especially the stateless-router claim versus sliding-window histograms and the stated principal driver of gains versus the router contribution.
rationale.R1.3: Strengthening the passage requires checking that the empirical claims, latency improvements, memory-pressure result, and causal attribution are supported by the reported experiments as written.
rationale.R1.5: The narrative arc likely needs reorganization because the advisor mode and its claimed role in latency improvements appear after the router has been presented as the central contribution.
rationale.R1.6: The delegation naturally includes improving sentence-level force, concision, and register to make the research pitch more compelling.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of this broad delegation should preserve uncertainty about which kind of strengthening is intended where the request is underspecified.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact makes strong causal and contribution claims that require overclaim control during any strengthening pass.
rationale.RX.3: The responsibility is limited to projecting the delegation onto paper-writing dimensions rather than executing edits or evaluating the system itself.
rationale.RX.4: Strengthening should avoid producing misleading research claims that could distort downstream scientific interpretation or operator decisions.
rationale.RX.5: Traceability is needed so each active responsibility can be grounded in specific tensions and claims in the provided artifact.
